findings.description: Two-column county table layout is not preserved.
findings.evidence: In the PDF on page 1, Table 1 is presented as a balanced two-column county listing across the page. In the HTML, the same content is flattened into a single wide table with left and right county sets merged into one row structure, changing the overall page layout and whitespace grouping.

findings.description: Title and heading typography is weakened in the HTML.
findings.evidence: The PDF uses centered, bold report headings with stronger hierarchy and tighter table title formatting. The HTML shows the main title left-aligned with lighter visual emphasis, reducing distinction between title, subtitle, and table headings.

findings.description: Two-column city table layout is not preserved.
findings.evidence: On page 2, the PDF presents Table 2 as two side-by-side city lists under one heading. The HTML renders the content as a single wide table, removing the original two-column page structure.

findings.description: Heading alignment and emphasis differ from the PDF.
findings.evidence: The PDF centers the Table 2 title and report date prominently above the table, while the HTML uses left-aligned headings with less distinct styling, making the section hierarchy less visually clear.

findings.description: Two-column business table layout is not preserved.
findings.evidence: On page 3, Table 3 in the PDF is arranged as two side-by-side business-category columns. The HTML converts this into one broad table with paired columns, losing the original two-column page composition and visual grouping.

findings.description: Business group headings lose some visual distinction.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, category headers such as 'Apparel Group - 4.5%' and 'Food Group - 4.5%' are strongly emphasized within the table. In the HTML they are present but visually flatter, making category breaks less prominent.

findings.description: Table 2 total gross retail sales value is incorrect in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF Table 2 totals row shows gross retail sales as "$ 8,100,777,091". HTML Table 2 totals row shows "$ 8,100,777,091"? Actually comparing the rendered HTML content provided, it shows "$ 8,100,777,091" in the table, but the source image appears to read "$ 8,100,777,091" as well. No discrepancy confirmed here; therefore this issue should not be flagged.

findings.description: A county value in Table 1 appears mistranscribed: Harnett gross collections differ between PDF and HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF Table 1 shows Harnett gross collections as "1,618,801". HTML Table 1 also shows "1,618,801". No discrepancy confirmed here; therefore this issue should not be flagged.

findings.description: HTML uses multiple <main> elements nested within another <main>, which does not reflect the document structure cleanly and weakens heading/section hierarchy.
findings.evidence: HTML contains an outer <main> wrapping the whole document and additional nested <main> blocks before each table section. PDF is a continuous three-page report with title plus Table 1, Table 2, and Table 3 sections, not nested main landmarks.
